note: Alaska's interstates (A-1 through A-4) are unsigned routes; drivers know them by highway names (Glenn, Richardson, Alaska, Seward/Sterling, and Parks Highways) and by highway mileposts, not interstate exits. 'mm' values below are route-relative and approximate; notes give the conventional highway milepost where known. Services are sparse -- fuel up at Anchorage, Fairbanks, Tok, Glennallen, and Delta Junction. Alaska DOT&PF maintains scattered highway waysides and pullouts rather than a formal rest-area system; no public rest areas with designated truck parking exist on these routes, so none are listed.

stops.name: Weigh Station (SB)
stops.notes: Alaska DOT&PF CVE scale on the Glenn Hwy at Eagle River (~MP 13); serves Anchorage-bound traffic

stops.name: The Hub of Alaska
stops.notes: Glenn Hwy milepost 189 at Richardson Hwy junction; fuel, parking, store

stops.name: Tok junction fuel stops (Young's Chevron / Three Bears Outpost)
stops.notes: Alaska Hwy milepost ~1314; main fuel/parking hub between the border and Fairbanks

stops.name: Delta Petro-Wash
stops.notes: Alaska Hwy milepost 1422 area (end of Alaska Hwy); fuel, truck wash, parking

stops.name: Sourdough Fuel (Petro Star)
stops.notes: Fairbanks terminus; Hilltop Truckstop (Fox, Steese Hwy mile 5.5) just north is the traditional Dalton Hwy jump-off

stops.name: Potter Weigh Station (both directions)
stops.notes: Alaska DOT&PF CVE scale at Seward Hwy milepost 114.5 (Potter, south edge of Anchorage); mm is route-relative from Anchorage

stops.name: Ester Weigh Station (both directions)
stops.notes: Alaska DOT&PF scale on the Parks Hwy near Ester (~MP 351), west of Fairbanks; operation reported intermittent
